title: Sharing Photos
drDigitalIntro: Sharing a photo sends a copy to someone else — via email, text message, social media, or other apps. You don't lose your photo; they get a copy. The photo file is attached to a message, email, or post. Photo quality can vary — sending over email may compress the image. For the best quality, use a cloud photo link or send from a photo app directly.

steps.say: Click on the Garden Flowers photo to open it.
Click the Share button to send this photo to someone.

drDigitalSuccess: You shared a photo! Sharing sends a copy — your original photo stays safe in your library.
drDigitalHint: Open a photo, then click the Share button (it looks like an arrow pointing up).
